wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the BGTS web application. Cover project overview, prerequisites (Node.js 18+, npm/yarn/pnpm), step-by-step installation process, environment variable configuration, development server setup, and production build/deployment. Include practical examples of running the application locally, accessing different locales (/tr/ and /tr/en/), and verifying the setup works correctly. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and explain the project's purpose as a corporate web platform for BGTS.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Next.js App Router architecture and routing system. Explain the file-based routing structure, dynamic route handling with [lang] parameter, locale-aware URL generation, and route resolution patterns. Document the middleware implementation for request processing, URL rewriting, and locale detection. Detail the relationship between routes.ts utility functions and the main layout system. Include examples of route generation, navigation patterns, and URL localization strategies. Address performance considerations and caching mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Locale Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the locale configuration system in the BGTS web application. Explain the locale definition structure, supported languages (Turkish and English), default locale selection, and locale detection mechanisms. Document the locale-aware URL structure with Turkish (/tr/) and English (/en/) prefixes, automatic locale detection from browser preferences, and fallback behavior. Include technical details of the locale configuration object, route generation helpers, and URL manipulation utilities. Provide examples of configuring new locales and customizing locale behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Root Layout Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the root layout structure of the BGTS web application. Document the main layout component that wraps all pages, including global styles, meta tags, and shared providers. Explain the internationalization wrapper, middleware integration, and how the layout coordinates with child components. Detail the component hierarchy, prop forwarding, and how the layout maintains consistency across different locales. Include examples of layout customization and extending the root layout for new pages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Content System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the page content system used throughout the BGTS website. Explain how TypeScript objects serve as the content management system, detailing the structure and organization of content files for different pages including home page content, software development services content, and managed services content. Document the content object schema, field definitions, and how content is integrated with page components. Include examples of content structure patterns, content updates procedures, and best practices for maintaining consistency across different page types. Address content versioning, localization considerations, and content delivery mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Primitives
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the UI primitive components that form the foundation of the BGTS design system. Document Container, Section, Typography, Button, and Breadcrumb components with their prop interfaces, styling approaches using Tailwind CSS v4, and usage patterns. Explain the Container component's responsive padding and element type flexibility, Section component's spacing and layout capabilities, Typography component's variant system and semantic usage, Button component's variants using Class Variance Authority (CVA), and Breadcrumb component's navigation patterns. Include practical examples showing component composition, customization options, accessibility features, and integration with other components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contact Form System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the contact form system implementation. Document the React form component with Zod validation, form state management, error handling, and success feedback mechanisms. Explain the API route implementation for processing form submissions, including rate limiting middleware, email sending via Gmail API, and security considerations. Include detailed examples of form validation rules, error messages, success responses, and integration patterns. Document the email template structure, recipient configuration, and fallback mechanisms for failed deliveries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Metadata Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the metadata management system in the BGTS web application. Document the SEO library implementation with locale-aware meta tags, title generation with fallback mechanisms, description optimization for different page types, and Open Graph/ Twitter Card configurations. Explain how metadata is dynamically generated based on page content and locale, the role of the root layout in providing default metadata, and best practices for optimizing SEO metadata across different page templates. Include examples of metadata configuration for various page types including product pages, service pages, and landing pages.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Chatbot System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AI chatbot system implementation. Document the ChatbotWidget component architecture, Groq API integration patterns, message handling workflows, and conversation state management. Explain the server-side API route implementation, request/response processing, and error handling mechanisms. Include configuration options for chatbot behavior, customization of responses, integration with external AI services, and troubleshooting common chatbot issues. Provide examples of extending chatbot functionality and implementing custom conversation flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Corporate Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for corporate pages including the homepage, about us, culture, social contribution, contact, partnerships, and learning pages. Document the implementation of each corporate page, their specific content structure, SEO optimization strategies, and integration with the overall site architecture. Explain the content management approach for corporate messaging, the role of each page in the customer journey, and how they contribute to brand storytelling. Include implementation details for contact forms, cultural showcases, and partnership presentations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the BGTS web application. Describe the Next.js 16 App Router architecture, internationalization system with locale-aware routing, middleware implementation for routing and rate limiting, server components pattern, and component composition architecture. Explain the overall system design, data flow patterns, API route structure, and integration points. Document the technology stack choices, security measures including HTTP headers and rate limiting, and deployment considerations. Include architectural diagrams showing component relationships and request flow patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internationalization Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the internationalization (i18n) architecture. Explain the locale configuration system, dictionary loading mechanism, and dynamic language switching. Document the base path handling for different locales, URL structure for localized content, and translation key management. Detail the integration between i18n-config.ts, get-dictionary.ts, and the JSON dictionary files. Include examples of implementing new languages, adding translation keys, and handling fallback languages. Address performance optimization for dictionary loading and caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dictionary System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the dictionary-based translation system. Detail the JSON-based dictionary structure, content organization, and dynamic loading mechanism. Explain the get-dictionary.ts implementation, dictionary caching strategy, and locale-specific content loading. Document the translation key naming conventions, nested object structure for hierarchical content, and fallback mechanisms for missing translations. Include practical examples of adding new translation keys, organizing content hierarchies, and implementing dynamic content updates. Address performance considerations and memory management for large dictionaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Header & Navigation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the header and navigation system. Document the sticky header component, navigation menu structure, and mega menu implementation for services, products, industries, and resources. Explain the navigation data structure, menu item configuration, hover/focus states, and keyboard navigation support. Detail the responsive breakpoint handling, menu positioning, and integration with the internationalization system. Include examples of customizing menu items and adding new navigation categories.

wiki_catalogs.name: Case Study Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the case study management system covering both Turkish and English content versions. Explain the data structure for case studies, including project details, client information, technologies used, and outcomes achieved. Document the bilingual content strategy, synchronization between Turkish and English versions, and how case studies are filtered and displayed. Include examples of adding new case studies, updating existing ones, and managing content across different languages. Address the relationship between case study data and the CaseStudyCard component, content organization patterns, and best practices for maintaining case study quality and consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for content-focused components that display dynamic information and engage users. Document Hero component for prominent landing content, ContentSection for structured content blocks, MetricCard for statistics display, TestimonialCard for customer feedback presentation, and AnimatedElements for interactive animations. Explain component props, content formatting options, responsive behavior, animation configurations, and integration patterns. Include examples of content binding, dynamic data handling, accessibility considerations, and performance optimizations for large content sets.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Chat API Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AI chat API endpoint implementation. Document the Vercel AI SDK integration, Groq API configuration, streaming response handling, and conversation state management. Explain the request/response schemas, authentication requirements, rate limiting strategies, and error handling patterns. Include examples of chat interactions, message formatting, context preservation, and integration with the frontend chat widget. Document security considerations, API key management, and performance optimization techniques for real-time chat functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Structured Data & Schema Markup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the structured data and schema markup implementation in the BGTS application. Document the JSON-LD schema generation for organizations, products, services, and articles, the implementation of Google Rich Results integration, and the dynamic generation of structured data based on page context. Explain the schema.org compliance, the relationship between structured data and SEO performance, and how structured data enhances search engine visibility. Include examples of different schema types used across various page templates and best practices for maintaining schema consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cookie Consent System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the cookie consent system implementation. Document the CookieConsent component functionality, GDPR compliance features, cookie categorization (necessary vs. analytics), and user preference management. Explain the cookie consent logic, storage mechanisms, and integration with external services. Include configuration options for cookie types, customization of consent messages, and implementation of consent withdrawal procedures. Provide examples of adding new cookie categories and troubleshooting consent-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Services Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for services pages covering software development, managed services, and DevOps offerings. Document the service-specific implementations, client-side interactivity patterns, and content presentation strategies. Explain how each service page integrates with the broader services navigation, the role of client components for interactive features, and the content structure for technical service descriptions. Include implementation details for service-specific UI components, data presentation patterns, and user engagement features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internationalization (i18n)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive internationalization documentation for the BGTS web application. Detail the locale-aware URL structure with Turkish (/tr/) and English (/tr/en/) prefixes, the JSON-based dictionary system, middleware-driven locale routing, and automatic URL redirection for legacy paths. Explain the locale configuration, dictionary loading mechanism, route localization helpers, and SEO considerations including hreflang implementation. Document the technical implementation of locale switching, URL generation helpers, and content management for multilingual pages. Include practical examples of adding new languages and managing translations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the middleware implementation and security architecture. Explain the request processing pipeline, locale detection and redirection, rate limiting mechanisms, and security headers configuration. Document the cookie consent system integration, GDPR compliance measures, and data protection features. Detail the middleware execution order, request transformation patterns, and response modification strategies. Include examples of custom middleware implementation, security rule configuration, and performance monitoring. Address deployment considerations and security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: URL Routing & Localization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for URL routing and localization mechanisms. Explain the locale-aware routing system, URL prefix handling, and automatic locale detection from URLs. Document the route generation helpers, URL construction utilities, and path manipulation functions. Detail the middleware implementation for locale routing, URL redirection logic, and legacy path handling. Include examples of generating localized URLs, handling route parameters, and implementing custom routing logic. Address SEO considerations including hreflang implementation, canonical URL generation, and international domain configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mobile Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the mobile navigation system. Document the mobile navigation component with accordion-style menu structure, hamburger menu trigger, and responsive breakpoint handling. Explain the menu expansion/collapse animation, touch gesture support, and accessibility features for mobile devices. Detail the integration with the desktop mega menus and how content is adapted for smaller screens. Include examples of customizing mobile menu behavior and adding mobile-specific navigation items.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Rendering Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for content rendering patterns and delivery mechanisms in the BGTS application. Explain how content objects are loaded, processed, and rendered in React components, including the integration between server components and client components. Document the content delivery pipeline, caching strategies, and performance optimizations. Include examples of content rendering workflows, component composition patterns, and how content is localized and adapted for different pages. Address content hydration, SSR considerations, and best practices for efficient content delivery across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specialized Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for specialized components designed for specific business use cases. Document CaseStudyCard for project showcase, CultureCard for employee stories, TechStackGrid for technology displays, ZoomableImage for interactive media, and GradientMesh for visual effects. Explain component-specific props, data binding requirements, custom styling options, and integration with business logic. Include examples of data formatting, interactive behaviors, responsive design considerations, and performance optimizations for media-heavy components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rate Limiting & Security Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the rate limiting and security middleware implementation. Document the middleware configuration, IP-based rate limiting, request throttling strategies, and security headers. Explain the middleware execution order, request tracking mechanisms, and integration with external services. Include examples of rate limit configurations, custom middleware patterns, and security best practices. Document the relationship between middleware and API endpoints, error handling for rate limit violations, and monitoring approaches for abuse prevention.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dynamic Sitemap Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the dynamic sitemap generation system in the BGTS application. Document the sitemap.ts implementation with locale-aware URL generation, hreflang attribute configuration for internationalization, priority and changefreq calculations, and XML sitemap formatting. Explain how the sitemap integrates with the internationalization system, handles dynamic routes, and optimizes for search engine crawling. Include examples of sitemap structure, configuration options, and best practices for maintaining sitemap accuracy across different locales and content updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Google Analytics integration system. Document the conditional loading mechanism, event tracking implementation, page view tracking, and conversion measurement. Explain the analytics configuration options, data collection controls, and privacy-compliant tracking practices. Include examples of custom event tracking, user behavior analysis, and integration with other analytics platforms. Provide troubleshooting guidance for tracking issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Industries Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for industry-specific pages covering banking, defense, telecommunications, retail, and other sectors. Document the specialized content presentation for each industry vertical, the client-side interactivity patterns for sector-specific demonstrations, and the content management approach for industry case studies. Explain how industry pages adapt the general services approach to sector-specific needs, the implementation of industry-specific UI patterns, and the integration with the broader industries navigation structure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the layout and navigation system of the BGTS web application. Document the root layout structure, header component with sticky navigation and mega menus, mobile navigation with accordion functionality, footer with corporate information, and global breadcrumb system. Explain the navigation hierarchy, mega menu implementation for services, products, industries, and resources, search overlay functionality, and responsive design patterns. Include component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and integration with the internationalization system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the component architecture and composition patterns. Explain the server component pattern, client component integration, and component hierarchy structure. Document the layout system with global components, reusable UI primitives, and specialized content components. Detail the component composition patterns, prop drilling prevention, and state management strategies. Include examples of component creation, reusability patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Address component testing strategies and accessibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multilingual Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for managing multilingual content across the application. Explain the file-based content organization with language-specific directories, content structure patterns, and component integration. Document the content loading mechanism, locale-aware content rendering, and fallback strategies for missing content. Detail the content types used (home.ts, software-development.ts, managed-services.ts), content validation, and update workflows. Include practical examples of adding new content sections, managing content hierarchies, and implementing content preview systems. Address content synchronization between languages and version management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Footer System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the footer system. Document the corporate footer component with contact information, social media links, quick links, and legal notices. Explain the footer layout structure, responsive grid system, and content organization patterns. Detail the integration with the internationalization system for localized content and how footer sections adapt to different languages. Include examples of customizing footer content, adding new footer sections, and maintaining consistent branding across footer elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document layout and structural components that organize page content and manage navigation. Cover Container and Section components for page structure, ContentSection for content blocks, MegaMenus for complex navigation hierarchies, and SearchOverlay for search functionality. Explain layout patterns, responsive breakpoints, navigation behaviors, and integration with the overall page architecture. Include examples of layout composition, navigation state management, accessibility features for complex menus, and performance considerations for large navigation structures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for performance optimization strategies in the BGTS web application. Document Next.js performance configurations including image optimization, static generation, and caching strategies. Explain the implementation of lazy loading for images and components, CSS optimization techniques, bundle size reduction methods, and runtime performance monitoring. Cover security headers configuration, CDN integration possibilities, and performance metrics collection. Include practical examples of optimization implementations, performance measurement tools, and best practices for maintaining optimal page speed scores across different devices and network conditions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Structured Data & SEO Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the structured data and SEO enhancement features. Document the StructuredData component implementation, schema.org markup generation, and SEO metadata management. Explain the sitemap generation process, dynamic URL handling, and search engine optimization strategies. Include configuration options for different content types, customization of SEO attributes, and integration with external SEO tools. Provide examples of implementing rich snippets, optimizing meta tags, and troubleshooting SEO issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Products Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for product showcase pages covering all BGTS products including AI hiring assistant, Cortex, CV converter, Doc2Bot, DocMind, HCM platform, MeetSense, and Praxila. Document the product-specific implementations, interactive demonstration patterns, feature showcase approaches, and client-side interactivity for product previews. Explain how each product page presents technical capabilities, integrates with product-specific UI components, and provides user engagement through interactive elements. Include implementation details for product feature displays, demo integrations, and technical specification presentations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the BGTS component system. Document the UI primitive components including Container, Section, Typography, Button, Hero, and specialized components like CaseStudyCard, CultureCard, and TechStackGrid. Explain the component composition patterns, prop interfaces, styling approaches using Tailwind CSS v4, and the class variance authority (CVA) system for button variants. Include usage examples, customization options, accessibility considerations, and integration patterns with other components. Document the design system principles and component inheritance patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the backend endpoints and integration patterns. Document the RESTful API structure, request/response schemas, and error handling strategies. Detail the contact form processing API, AI chat functionality, and email integration services. Explain the API route organization, middleware integration, and security measures. Include examples of API consumption, authentication patterns, and rate limiting. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and performance optimization. Document integration with external services like Google APIs and Groq AI.

wiki_catalogs.name: Breadcrumb System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the breadcrumb navigation system. Document the global breadcrumb component, dynamic breadcrumb generation based on current route, and integration with the routing system. Explain the breadcrumb data structure, URL mapping, and how breadcrumbs reflect the hierarchical navigation structure. Detail the responsive behavior, accessibility features, and styling patterns. Include examples of customizing breadcrumb appearance, adding custom breadcrumb paths, and handling special routing scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Resources Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for resources pages covering success stories and infographics. Document the content presentation strategies for case studies, the client-side filtering and display mechanisms, and the infographic showcase implementation. Explain how resources pages present quantitative achievements, customer testimonials, and visual content. Include implementation details for case study filtering systems, interactive content displays, and the integration of multimedia resources with the overall content strategy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the content management system of the BGTS web application. Explain the headless CMS approach using TypeScript objects for content management, the structure of content files for different pages, case study data management with Turkish and English versions, and the content delivery patterns. Document how content is organized, updated, and rendered in components, the relationship between content objects and page components, and best practices for content authoring and maintenance. Include examples of adding new content sections and managing multilingual content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search Overlay System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the search overlay system. Document the search overlay component, search functionality implementation, and search data structure. Explain the overlay trigger mechanism, search input handling, result filtering, and navigation to search results. Detail the search indexing strategy, performance optimizations, and integration with the content management system. Include examples of customizing search behavior, adding search filters, and improving search result relevance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Career & Learning Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for career and learning pages including career paths and learning & development sections. Document the implementation of career progression visualization, skill development pathways, and educational resource presentation. Explain how these pages integrate with the company's talent development initiatives, the client-side interactivity for career exploration, and the content structure for learning resources. Include implementation details for career path visualization, learning module organization, and user engagement patterns specific to talent development content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pages & Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the page structure and routing system of the BGTS web application. Document the Next.js App Router file structure, dynamic routing with locale segments, page component organization, and URL mapping between internal routes and visible URLs. Explain the hierarchical page structure covering corporate pages, services, industries, products, and resources. Include routing patterns, navigation between pages, SEO considerations for each page type, and best practices for extending the page structure with new sections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Forms & API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the forms and API endpoints system of the BGTS web application. Document the contact form implementation with Zod validation, Gmail API integration for email processing, rate limiting middleware, and the AI chatbot API endpoint. Explain the form validation patterns, error handling, success feedback mechanisms, and security considerations including rate limiting and input sanitization. Include API endpoint specifications, request/response schemas, and integration examples for extending the form system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Features & Enhancements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the enhanced features of the BGTS web application. Document the AI chatbot widget system with Groq API integration, cookie consent banner with GDPR compliance, Google Analytics integration with conditional loading, and structured data implementation for SEO. Explain the technical implementation of each feature, configuration options, activation/deactivation processes, and integration with the overall system architecture. Include examples of customizing feature behavior and troubleshooting common issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: SEO & Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the SEO and performance optimization systems of the BGTS web application. Document the metadata generation system with locale-aware titles and descriptions, structured data implementation using JSON-LD, dynamic sitemap generation with hreflang support, and performance optimizations including image optimization and security headers. Explain the technical implementation of SEO features, performance monitoring approaches, and best practices for maintaining optimal SEO rankings and page speed scores.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the testing and quality assurance systems of the BGTS web application. Document the Vitest testing setup, component testing patterns using React Testing Library, test configuration, and linting rules with ESLint. Explain testing strategies for different component types, unit testing approaches, integration testing considerations, and continuous integration practices. Include guidelines for writing effective tests, maintaining test coverage, and ensuring code quality standards across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Build
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the deployment and build processes of the BGTS web application. Document the Next.js build configuration, PostCSS and Tailwind CSS setup, Vercel deployment process with environment variables, and alternative deployment options including Plesk. Explain the build optimization strategies, environment variable management, security configurations, and deployment best practices. Include troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# BGTS Web Project - Comprehensive Technical Overview

## Project Introduction

**BGTS (Business & Global Technology Solutions)** is a corporate web portal designed to showcase a technology company's services, products, and expertise across multiple international markets. Built with modern web technologies, the platform serves as a comprehensive digital presence supporting both Turkish and English-speaking audiences.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Multi-language Corporate Presence**: Deliver seamless bilingual experience (Turkish/English) for global business operations
- **Service Showcase**: Present technology solutions including software development, DevOps, managed services, and AI-powered products
- **Lead Generation**: Facilitate business inquiries through integrated contact forms and application systems
- **Brand Consistency**: Maintain professional corporate identity across all digital touchpoints
- **Technical Excellence**: Demonstrate cutting-edge web development practices and scalability

### Target Audience

- **Primary**: Potential clients seeking technology solutions in finance, defense, retail, and telecommunications sectors
- **Secondary**: Job candidates interested in career opportunities within the technology ecosystem
- **Internal**: Company stakeholders requiring content management and analytics capabilities

## Technical Architecture

The BGTS web application follows a modern Next.js 16 architecture with comprehensive internationalization support and enterprise-grade security features.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[Middleware Layer]
    C --> D[Locale Detection]
    C --> E[Route Resolution]
    B --> F[Server Components]
    F --> G[Static Content]
    F --> H[Dynamic Data]
    B --> I[Client Components]
    I --> J[Interactive Forms]
    I --> K[AI Chat Interface]
    B --> L[API Routes]
    L --> M[Contact Forms]
    L --> N[AI Chat]
    L --> O[File Uploads]
    B --> P[Static Assets]
    P --> Q[Images]
    P --> R[Fonts]
    P --> S[Icons]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture implements a clear separation between server-side rendering for SEO optimization and client-side interactivity for enhanced user experience.

**Server-Side Components**:
- Dynamic content generation with automatic metadata optimization
- Internationalization handling with locale-aware routing
- SEO-friendly structured data implementation
- Performance-optimized image delivery

**Client-Side Components**:
- Interactive forms with real-time validation
- AI-powered chat interface
- Animated UI elements with smooth transitions
- Responsive navigation with mega-menu functionality

### Design Patterns

The project employs several key architectural patterns:

- **Server Component Pattern**: Optimizes performance by rendering most content server-side
- **Internationalization Pattern**: Implements locale-aware routing with automatic fallback mechanisms
- **Component Composition Pattern**: Reusable UI components with consistent styling
- **API Route Pattern**: Secure backend endpoints for form processing and AI interactions

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Root Layout Configuration**
- Location: `src/app/[lang]/layout.tsx`
- Handles global layout structure with header, footer, and breadcrumb navigation
- Implements dynamic language switching and SEO metadata generation

**Homepage Implementation**
- Location: `src/app/[lang]/page.tsx`
- Features hero slider carousel with animated transitions
- Integrates multiple content sections with responsive design

### Core Modules

**Internationalization System**
- Configuration: `src/i18n-config.ts`
- Dictionary management: `src/get-dictionary.ts`
- Multi-language content resolution with automatic fallback

**SEO Optimization Framework**
- Metadata generation: `src/app/[lang]/layout.tsx`
- Structured data implementation: `src/components/seo/StructuredData.tsx`
- hreflang tag management: `src/lib/seo.ts`

**Content Management System**
- Dynamic content loading: `src/content/home.ts`
- Case study integration: `src/data/case-studies.tr.ts`
- Flexible content structure supporting multiple page types

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration**
- Next.js 16 with App Router architecture
- TypeScript 5 for type safety
- Tailwind CSS 4 for utility-first styling
- PostCSS integration for advanced CSS features

**Environment Management**
- Runtime configuration through environment variables
- API key management for external services
- Email configuration for contact form processing

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies**:
- Next.js 16.1.1 - Modern React framework with App Router
- React 19.2.3 - Component-based UI library
- TypeScript 5 - Type-safe JavaScript development

**UI and Styling**:
- Tailwind CSS 4 - Utility-first CSS framework
- Framer Motion 12 - Animation library for smooth transitions
- Lucide React - SVG icon library for consistent visual elements

**Form and Validation**:
- React Hook Form 7.69.0 - High-performance form handling
- Zod 4.3.2 - TypeScript-first schema validation
- Class Variance Authority 0.7.1 - Component variant system

**Advanced Features**:
- Vercel AI SDK 3.4.33 - AI-powered chat functionality
- Google APIs 173.0.0 - Integration with Google services
- Nodemailer 8.0.1 - Email service integration

### Integration Points

**AI Chat Integration**
- Groq API integration for conversational AI
- Real-time chat interface with conversation history
- Rate limiting and performance optimization

**Email Service Integration**
- SMTP configuration for automated email responses
- HTML template generation for contact form submissions
- GDPR-compliant data handling and consent management

**Analytics Integration**
- Google Analytics implementation
- Conversion tracking for lead generation
- Performance monitoring and user behavior analysis

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Root Layout] --> B[Header Component]
    A --> C[Footer Component]
    A --> D[Main Content]
    D --> E[Page Components]
    E --> F[Content Sections]
    F --> G[UI Components]
    G --> H[Reusable Elements]
    
    B --> I[Mega Menus]
    B --> J[Language Switcher]
    B --> K[Search Overlay]
    
    D --> L[Contact Form]
    D --> M[Product Showcase]
    D --> N[Service Pages]
    
    L --> O[Form Validation]
    L --> P[Email Processing]
    P --> Q[SMTP Integration]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Language Support**
- Seamless Turkish and English content delivery
- Automatic locale detection and routing
- Dynamic content translation with dictionary system
- SEO-optimized hreflang implementation

**Interactive Experience**
- Hero slider with auto-rotation and manual controls
- Animated transitions using Framer Motion
- Responsive navigation with mobile optimization
- Real-time form validation with user feedback

**Business Intelligence**
- Lead capture through integrated contact forms
- Career application processing system
- Product demonstration interfaces
- Industry-specific service showcases

### Implementation Highlights

**Performance Optimization**
- Server-side rendering for improved SEO and loading speeds
- Image optimization with Next.js Image component
- Code splitting for efficient bundle loading
- Lazy loading for non-critical resources

**Security Implementation**
- Rate limiting for API endpoints
- Input sanitization for form submissions
- HTTPS enforcement and security headers
- GDPR compliance for data protection

**Accessibility Features**
- Semantic HTML structure throughout
- ARIA labels for screen reader compatibility
- Keyboard navigation support
- Color contrast optimization for readability

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> ContentReady: Data Fetched
    ContentReady --> UserInteraction: Component Mounted
    UserInteraction --> FormSubmission: Contact Form Submit
    UserInteraction --> ChatInteraction: AI Chat Start
    FormSubmission --> EmailProcessing: Validation Passed
    EmailProcessing --> Success: Email Sent
    EmailProcessing --> Error: Validation Failed
    Success --> ContentReady: Reset Form
    Error --> UserInteraction: Show Errors
    ChatInteraction --> AIResponse: Message Processed
    AIResponse --> UserInteraction: Continue Conversation
```

The BGTS web project represents a comprehensive solution for corporate digital presence, combining modern web technologies with enterprise-grade functionality. Its modular architecture supports scalable growth while maintaining optimal performance and user experience across multiple international markets.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [src/app/[lang]/layout.tsx](src/app/%5Blang%5D/layout.tsx)
- [src/app/[lang]/page.tsx](src/app/%5Blang%5D/page.tsx)
- [src/components/layout/Header.tsx](src/components/layout/Header.tsx)

- [PROJECT_ANALYSIS.md](PROJECT_ANALYSIS.md)

</blog>
